children.code: /* Raw fetch: up to 8 bytes from CS:IP (enough for 2 prefix + 6-byte instruction) */
/* ModR/M instruction length (extra bytes consumed by ModR/M addressing) */

/* Direct address segment: DS unless segment override is active.
     Used by MOV AL/AX,[addr] and MOV [addr],AL/AX (opcodes 0xA0-0xA3). */

/* Effective address computation.
     If a segment override prefix is active, use the override segment instead of default. */

/* Immediate values after ModR/M (position depends on modrmExtra).
     immOff = prefixLen + 2 + modrmExtra (offset from raw ipAddr). */
/* Immediate value at opcode+1 (for MOV reg,imm and other non-ModR/M immediates) */

/* TF (Trap Flag) single-step: 8086 fires INT 1 AFTER the instruction completes.
     --_tfPending: set to 1 when current FLAGS has TF=1 (latches the request).
     --_tf: fires INT 1 on the NEXT tick by reading __1_tfPending.
     This way the instruction executes normally, then INT 1 fires on the following tick. */
--haltCode: calc(var(--unknownOp) * var(--opcode)); /* the offending opcode, 0 while running */
